prompt: I want to be able to jump back to recently-edited files in nvim without leaving home row. Currently I'm doing :e with tab-completion which is annoying.

assertions.check: with_skill/outputs/session.json exists and has a non-empty session_id, intent matches the user's phrasing
session.json.suggestions has at least 3 entries, each with id/type/title/rationale
suggestion types include all of: existing, addon, new
at least one suggestion references that fzf is already in tools/fzf/ but not wired into nvim, OR explains the gap between current state and desired outcome
at least one suggestion names a specific, real nvim plugin that solves the stated problem (e.g. telescope.nvim, harpoon, fzf-lua, oil.nvim, mini.pick)
session.json.trials is empty — Claude did not try to install anything
suggestions.md is present, grouped by type, each entry has a one-line rationale
